title: Leucopogon kingianus
taxonomy: Leucopogon kingianus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to Australia and is a member of the genus Leucopogon, which consists of over 120 species of plants. Leucopogon kingianus is commonly known as King's beard and is a shrub that grows up to 1.5 meters in height. The plant is found in a variety of habitats, including coastal areas and mountainous regions. It is an important component of the Australian flora and is highly valued for its unique characteristics and uses.
